seoTitle: Hawun Capacitor Solutions | Power Supply & LED Driver Applications
seoDescription: Explore Hawun capacitor solutions for power supplies, LED drivers, and motor drives. Complete capacitor solutions with technical support from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Hawun capacitor solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Hawun capacitor solution involves: 1) Define your application requirements - power supply, LED driver, motor drive, or inverter; 2) Identify operating conditions - voltage, current, temperature, and lifetime requirements; 3) Choose capacitor type - aluminum electrolytic for bulk capacitance, film for filtering, solid for low ESR; 4) Calculate capacitance and voltage ratings with appropriate margins; 5) Consider size constraints and mounting options. Our FAE team can help assess your requirements and recommend the optimal capacitor configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for capacitor solution assessment and configuration recommendations.

faqs.question: What support does LiTong provide for Hawun solution implementation?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive support for Hawun capacitor solutions: Technical consultation - capacitor selection, ripple current calculations, lifetime analysis; Application engineering - schematic review, PCB layout guidance for capacitor placement; Prototype support - sample kits, evaluation boards, and testing assistance; Production support - BOM optimization, supply chain management, and quality assurance. Our FAE team has extensive experience with capacitor applications and can guide you through the entire design process from concept to production.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for solution implementation assistance.

faqs.question: Can Hawun capacitor solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hawun capacitor solutions can be customized to meet specific requirements: Standard capacitors can be selected with special voltage/capacitance combinations; Custom form factors available for volume applications; Special testing and screening for high-reliability applications; Tape and reel packaging for automated assembly. Customization process: Define requirements with our FAE team; Review proposed capacitor configuration; Validate with prototypes and testing; Transition to production with full support. Contact us to discuss your specific customization needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss customization options for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Hawun capacitor solutions?
faqs.answer: Lead times for Hawun capacitor solutions vary based on configuration: Standard catalog products: 2-4 weeks from factory; Special specifications: 4-6 weeks including testing; High-volume production: 3-5 weeks with scheduled deliveries; Samples and small quantities: 1-2 weeks from distributor stock. As an authorized distributor, LiTong maintains inventory of popular Hawun capacitor series for immediate delivery. For production programs, we recommend establishing forecasts and supply agreements to ensure continuous availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current lead times and project timeline planning.

faqs.question: How do Hawun capacitor solutions compare to other brands?
faqs.answer: Hawun capacitor solutions offer competitive advantages: Cost effectiveness - 20-40% lower cost than premium brands with comparable performance; Quality consistency - ISO 9001 certified manufacturing with strict process controls; Wide product range - comprehensive portfolio covering all major capacitor types; Local support - China-based manufacturing enables faster response and shorter lead times; Customization flexibility - willing to accommodate special requirements for volume orders. While premium brands may offer slightly higher performance in extreme conditions, Hawun provides excellent value for mainstream applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for a detailed comparison of Hawun solutions vs other brands.

solutions.name: Power Supply Capacitor Solution
solutions.title: Power Supply Capacitor Solution
solutions.description: Complete capacitor solution for switch-mode power supplies and DC-DC converters
solutions.longDescription: The Power Supply Capacitor Solution provides a comprehensive selection of Hawun capacitors optimized for switch-mode power supply applications. This solution includes input filter capacitors, output filter capacitors, bulk capacitors, and decoupling capacitors carefully selected to meet the demanding requirements of modern power supplies. The capacitor configuration ensures low ripple voltage, high efficiency, and long lifetime under continuous operation.
benefits: Low ESR capacitors minimize power losses and heat generation
High ripple current capability ensures reliable operation
Long lifetime ratings reduce maintenance requirements
Comprehensive voltage ratings cover all application needs
Compact sizes enable high power density designs

coreAdvantages.title: Low ESR Design
coreAdvantages.description: Special low-ESR electrolytic and solid capacitors minimize power dissipation and improve efficiency.

coreAdvantages.title: High Ripple Current

coreAdvantages.title: Long Lifetime

coreAdvantages.title: Wide Voltage Range

Low-ESR capacitors for output ripple reduction

customerCases.challenge: Needed long-life capacitors for outdoor LED drivers operating at high ambient temperatures up to 75°C with 5-year warranty requirement.
customerCases.solution: Implemented HT series 105°C capacitors with 5000-hour rating. Calculated lifetime of 40000 hours at 75°C using Arrhenius equation.
customerCases.result: Achieved calculated lifetime exceeding 5-year warranty. Zero field failures in first 3 years of deployment. Customer expanded product line using same solution.

customerCases.challenge: Required high reliability power supply for factory automation with 24/7 operation and minimal maintenance access.
customerCases.solution: Designed with HT series capacitors and conservative derating. Implemented redundancy with parallel capacitor banks.
customerCases.result: MTBF exceeded 100000 hours. Maintenance interval extended to 5 years. Customer achieved significant cost savings on service calls.

faeInsights.insight: Power supply capacitor selection is critical for both performance and reliability. The key is understanding that capacitor lifetime is primarily determined by temperature - every 10°C reduction doubles the lifetime. For high-reliability designs, I always recommend 105°C rated capacitors even when 85°C would suffice, as this provides significant margin for unexpected thermal conditions. Additionally, proper derating - using capacitors at 70-80% of rated voltage - can extend lifetime by 2-4x.
faeInsights.logic: Power supply capacitor design follows these principles: First, calculate required capacitance based on ripple current and voltage requirements. Second, select voltage rating with at least 20% margin. Third, choose 105°C rated capacitors for reliability margin. Fourth, calculate expected lifetime using Arrhenius equation at actual operating temperature. Fifth, implement proper thermal management to keep capacitors cool.
keyTakeaways: Temperature is the primary factor affecting capacitor lifetime
Ripple current calculations are essential for proper sizing
Thermal management is as important as capacitor selection
commonPitfalls: Insufficient voltage derating leading to reduced lifetime
Undersizing capacitors for ripple current requirements
Poor thermal design causing excessive capacitor heating
Not accounting for capacitor aging in lifetime calculations
bestPractices: Use 105°C capacitors for industrial and automotive applications
Derate voltage by at least 20% from maximum working voltage
Calculate ripple current and verify capacitor capability
Provide adequate airflow or heatsinking for capacitors
Perform lifetime calculations using actual operating conditions
troubleshootingTips: If capacitors overheat, check ripple current and ventilation
High ESR indicates capacitor aging or damage
Bulging or venting indicates overvoltage or overtemperature

faqs.question: How do I calculate the required capacitance for my power supply?
faqs.answer: Output capacitance calculation depends on your ripple voltage requirement. Use the formula: C = Iload / (2 × fsw × Vripple), where Iload is maximum load current, fsw is switching frequency, and Vripple is acceptable peak-to-peak ripple voltage. For example, with 5A load, 100kHz switching, and 50mV ripple: C = 5 / (2 × 100000 × 0.05) = 500μF. Select the next standard value (470μF or 1000μF) with 20-30% margin for tolerance and aging.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed capacitance calculations including input filter, output filter, and hold-up time requirements.

faqs.question: What is the recommended voltage derating for capacitors?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice is to operate capacitors at 70-80% of rated voltage. For a 25V capacitor, maximum working voltage should be 17.5-20V. This derating provides multiple benefits: Extended lifetime - voltage derating can double or triple capacitor life; Improved reliability - reduced stress on dielectric; Better surge capability - margin for voltage transients; Lower leakage current - improved circuit performance. For high-reliability applications, consider 50% derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations based on your reliability requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select capacitors for high ripple current applications?
faqs.answer: High ripple current applications require careful capacitor selection to prevent overheating and premature failure. Key considerations include: Ripple current rating - select capacitors with rated ripple current at least 20% higher than actual application ripple; ESR (Equivalent Series Resistance) - lower ESR means less power dissipation (P = I² × ESR) and less self-heating; Multiple capacitors - use parallel configuration to share ripple current and reduce effective ESR; Temperature derating - ripple current capability decreases at higher ambient temperatures; Solid capacitors - consider conductive polymer solid capacitors for ultra-high ripple current applications due to their extremely low ESR. Always calculate self-heating from ripple current and ensure capacitor temperature stays within ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for ripple current calculations and capacitor selection for high-current applications.

faqs.question: What is the optimal capacitor configuration for switching power supplies?
faqs.answer: Optimal switching power supply capacitor configuration uses different capacitor types for different functions: Input filter - electrolytic capacitors (HW/HT series) for bulk energy storage and line frequency filtering; Output filter - hybrid configuration with solid capacitors (HS/HU series) for high-frequency ripple and electrolytics for bulk capacitance; Decoupling - film capacitors (HF/HP series) for high-frequency noise suppression; Bootstrap - small ceramic or film capacitors for gate drive circuits. This multi-layer approach provides: Cost-effective bulk capacitance from electrolytics; Ultra-low ESR for high-frequency ripple from solid capacitors; Excellent high-frequency performance from film capacitors. The specific configuration depends on power level, switching frequency, and ripple requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for optimized capacitor configurations for your specific power supply design.

faqs.question: How do I ensure long capacitor lifetime in power supply applications?
faqs.answer: Ensuring long capacitor lifetime in power supplies requires attention to multiple factors: Temperature management - keep capacitors cool through proper airflow, heatsinking, and avoiding placement near heat sources; Voltage derating - operate at 70-80% of rated voltage to reduce stress on dielectric; Ripple current management - ensure actual ripple current is within capacitor ratings at operating temperature; 105°C capacitors - use high-temperature rated capacitors for industrial applications; Conservative design - calculate expected lifetime using Arrhenius equation and design with 2-3x margin. Additional practices include: Regular thermal measurements during validation; Avoiding capacitor placement near switching devices; Using multiple capacitors in parallel to share current and reduce heating; Implementing thermal management such as thermal vias and copper pours.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations and reliability optimization for your power supply design.

solutions.name: LED Driver Capacitor Solution
solutions.title: LED Driver Capacitor Solution
solutions.description: High-reliability capacitor solution for LED drivers with extended lifetime requirements
solutions.longDescription: The LED Driver Capacitor Solution provides a comprehensive selection of Hawun capacitors specifically optimized for LED driver applications. LED drivers operate at high ambient temperatures in enclosed fixtures, requiring capacitors with extended temperature ratings and long lifetimes. This solution combines 105°C rated aluminum electrolytic capacitors for bulk storage, solid capacitors for high-frequency output filtering, and film capacitors for EMI suppression. The capacitor configuration ensures minimal ripple current, high efficiency, and 50000+ hour lifetime for LED luminaires with 5-year warranties.
benefits: 105°C rated capacitors handle high fixture temperatures
Low ESR solid capacitors minimize output ripple
Comprehensive solution covers all capacitor needs

coreAdvantages.title: High Temperature Rating
coreAdvantages.description: 105°C rated capacitors maintain reliability in enclosed fixtures with limited airflow.

coreAdvantages.title: Extended Lifetime
coreAdvantages.description: Long-life capacitors ensure LED driver reliability matching LED lifetime of 50000+ hours.

coreAdvantages.title: Low Output Ripple
coreAdvantages.description: Solid capacitors with ultra-low ESR provide clean DC output for flicker-free LED operation.

coreAdvantages.title: EMI Compliance
coreAdvantages.description: Film capacitors for input filtering ensure compliance with EMC regulations.

coreAdvantages.title: Compact Size
coreAdvantages.description: High capacitance density enables compact LED driver designs for space-constrained fixtures.

components.description: High-voltage electrolytic and film capacitors for AC input filtering
High-temperature electrolytic capacitors for energy storage
Ultra-low ESR solid capacitors for output ripple reduction

customerCases.challenge: Street lights operate in sealed enclosures with ambient temperatures reaching 75°C. Previous LED drivers failed after 2-3 years due to capacitor degradation, requiring expensive bucket truck maintenance.
customerCases.solution: Implemented HT series 105°C capacitors with calculated 50000 hour lifetime at 75°C. Used hybrid solid + electrolytic output configuration for low ripple and long life.
customerCases.result: Zero capacitor-related failures in 4 years of operation. Maintenance interval extended from 3 years to 10+ years. City saved $2M in maintenance costs over 5-year period.

customerCases.challenge: High-bay fixtures in manufacturing facilities experience ambient temperatures of 65-70°C with 24/7 operation. Standard 85°C capacitors were failing within warranty period.
customerCases.solution: Designed with HT series 105°C capacitors and conservative 50% voltage derating. Implemented thermal management to keep capacitor temperature below 80°C.
customerCases.result: Calculated capacitor lifetime exceeded 80000 hours at operating conditions. Product achieved 5-year warranty with confidence. Customer became market leader in high-reliability industrial lighting.

faeInsights.insight: LED driver capacitor selection is critical because these applications combine high temperature, long lifetime requirements, and limited maintenance access. The key insight is that capacitor lifetime decreases exponentially with temperature - a capacitor rated for 5000 hours at 105°C will last 40000 hours at 75°C. For LED drivers with 5-year warranties, I always recommend 105°C capacitors with conservative derating. The hybrid approach using solid capacitors for high-frequency ripple and electrolytics for bulk storage provides the best combination of performance and cost.
faeInsights.logic: LED driver capacitor design follows these principles: First, determine maximum ambient temperature in the fixture including solar heating. Second, select 105°C rated capacitors for temperature margin. Third, apply 50% voltage derating for extended lifetime. Fourth, calculate expected capacitor lifetime using Arrhenius equation at worst-case temperature. Fifth, implement hybrid solid + electrolytic configuration for optimal performance. Sixth, verify thermal design through testing under actual operating conditions.
keyTakeaways: LED fixture temperatures often exceed 70°C - use 105°C capacitors
Voltage derating significantly extends capacitor lifetime
Hybrid capacitor configurations optimize performance and cost
Calculate lifetime at worst-case operating temperature
Thermal management is critical for LED driver reliability
commonPitfalls: Using 85°C capacitors in high-temperature LED fixtures
Insufficient voltage derating for long lifetime requirements
Not accounting for solar heating in outdoor fixtures
Poor thermal design causing excessive capacitor heating
Undersizing output capacitors for ripple requirements
bestPractices: Use 105°C capacitors for all LED driver applications
Apply 50% voltage derating from rated voltage
Calculate lifetime at maximum expected fixture temperature
Implement hybrid solid + electrolytic output configuration
Perform thermal testing in actual fixture under full load
troubleshootingTips: If LEDs flicker, check output capacitor ESR and capacitance
Measure capacitor temperature during thermal testing
Verify input capacitors can handle line surge requirements

faqs.question: Why are 105°C capacitors necessary for LED drivers?
faqs.answer: 105°C capacitors are essential for LED drivers due to challenging thermal environments: Enclosed fixtures - LED drivers operate in sealed housings with limited airflow; LED heat - LEDs generate heat that raises internal fixture temperature; Solar heating - outdoor fixtures absorb solar radiation adding 10-20°C; Ambient叠加 - fixture temperature equals ambient plus LED heat plus solar gain. Temperature example: 40°C ambient + 20°C LED heat + 15°C solar = 75°C fixture temperature. At 75°C, an 85°C capacitor has only 10°C margin and accelerated aging. A 105°C capacitor has 30°C margin and 4x longer lifetime. For 5-year warranties, 105°C capacitors are essential for reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For all LED driver applications, specify 105°C rated capacitors to ensure warranty compliance and long-term reliability.

faqs.question: How do I calculate capacitor lifetime for LED driver applications?
faqs.answer: LED driver capacitor lifetime calculation: Step 1 - Determine maximum fixture temperature including ambient, LED heat, and solar heating. Step 2 - Calculate capacitor core temperature: Tcore = Tfixture + (Iripple² × ESR × Rthermal). Step 3 - Apply Arrhenius equation: Lactual = Lrated × 2^((Trated - Tcore)/10). Example: Capacitor rated 5000 hours @ 105°C; Maximum fixture temperature 75°C; Calculated core temperature 80°C; Lactual = 5000 × 2^((105-80)/10) = 5000 × 2^2.5 = 28284 hours. At 12 hours/day operation: 28284 / (12 × 365) = 6.5 years. For 5-year warranty, this provides adequate margin. Always use worst-case temperature and include design margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed lifetime calculations specific to your LED driver design and fixture thermal characteristics.

faqs.question: What is the optimal capacitor configuration for LED driver output filtering?
faqs.answer: Optimal LED driver output uses hybrid capacitor configuration: Solid capacitors (HS/HU series) - place closest to LEDs for high-frequency ripple filtering, ultra-low ESR handles switching frequency ripple, 2-4 capacitors in parallel for current sharing. Electrolytic capacitors (HT series) - provide bulk capacitance for energy storage, handle low-frequency ripple components, lower cost per μF than solid capacitors. Configuration example: 2x HU330-16 (330μF, 8mΩ) in parallel with 2x HT1000-25 (1000μF, 120mΩ). Solid capacitors handle high-frequency ripple above 100kHz, electrolytics handle line frequency and switching fundamental. This hybrid approach provides: Low output ripple (<1%); Cost-effective bulk capacitance; Optimal high-frequency performance; Extended lifetime from conservative loading.
faqs.decisionGuide: For LED driver output filtering, use hybrid solid + electrolytic configuration for optimal performance and cost.

faqs.question: How do I ensure EMI compliance for LED drivers?
faqs.answer: LED driver EMI compliance requires proper capacitor selection and placement: Input filtering - X-capacitors (line-to-line) and Y-capacitors (line-to-ground) for conducted EMI suppression; Film capacitors - polypropylene preferred for high-frequency filtering due to low ESR and ESL; Differential mode - use film capacitors across input lines, typically 0.1-0.47μF; Common mode - use Y-capacitors to ground, limited to 4700pF for safety; Output filtering - solid capacitors for differential mode, ferrite beads for common mode. Hawun solution includes: HP474-250 (0.47μF film) for differential mode input filtering; Additional Y-capacitors for common mode filtering; HS/HU series solid capacitors for output differential filtering. Proper PCB layout with minimized loop areas is equally important for EMI compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EMI filter design and capacitor selection for your specific LED driver requirements.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for LED driver capacitors?
faqs.answer: LED driver capacitor voltage derating recommendations: Input capacitors - operate at 50-60% of rated voltage for maximum lifetime; Output capacitors - operate at 60-70% of rated voltage; Derating formula - if Vworking = 24V, select Vrated = 24V / 0.6 = 40V, use next standard 50V. Benefits of derating: Lifetime extension - 50% derating can double or triple capacitor life; Improved reliability - reduced stress on dielectric; Better surge capability - margin for voltage transients; Lower leakage current - improved efficiency. Example for 24V output: Without derating - use 25V capacitor, lifetime 5000 hours @ 105°C; With 50% derating - use 50V capacitor, lifetime 15000+ hours @ 105°C. The small cost increase for higher voltage rating is easily justified by the lifetime improvement, especially for warranty-critical LED applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Apply 40-50% voltage derating for LED driver capacitors to maximize lifetime and ensure warranty compliance.

faqs.question: How do I select capacitors for outdoor LED fixtures?
faqs.answer: Outdoor LED fixture capacitor selection considerations: Temperature range - must operate from -40°C to +85°C ambient; Solar heating - fixture can reach 15-20°C above ambient in direct sun; Waterproofing - IP65 or higher rating required, affects heat dissipation; Thermal design - may need heat sinking or thermal interface material; Capacitor selection: Use 105°C rated capacitors minimum; Calculate lifetime at 85°C fixture temperature; Consider 50V capacitors for 24V outputs (50% derating); Use conformal coating or potting for moisture protection. Hawun recommendations: HT series for bulk storage - 105°C rating, long lifetime; HS/HU series for output - wide temperature range, stable ESR; HP series for input filtering - excellent temperature stability. For extreme environments, consider 125°C rated capacitors or additional thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: For outdoor LED fixtures, use 105°C capacitors with conservative derating and verify thermal design through testing.
